titleStmt.title: Letter from D. Mullally to the Under-Secretary, 21 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Denis Mullally

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Denis Mullally, Clerk of Bray Urban District Council, to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938), Under-Secretary for Ireland, concerning the retirement on health grounds of the Bray Harbour Master, Mr. Thomas D. Scraggs (b. 1859). Mullally supplies Mr. Scraggs’ date of birth, which has been requested.A handwritten note on the letter indicates the response from the Chief Secretary's Office, due to his age, Mr. Scraggs is not eligible for a pension, but there is provision for payment on health grounds, if proper medical certification is provided.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5620/12926

correspAction.placeName: Bray Urban District Council, Town Hall, Bray, County Wicklow, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
TELEPHONE BRAY 6
ADDRESS ALL COMMUNICATIONS TO
THE CLERK OF THE COUNCIL Bray Urban District Council Town Hall Bray 27thJuly 1916 Sir, In reply to your letter of 25 Inst., I beg to inform
you that the date of Mr Scragg's birth was 2nd April 1859. I am
Sir
Your obedient servant D Mullally Clerk to the Council The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle, Dublin Ack. V reply that as Mr Scraggs has not
attained the age of 60 years he is ineligible
for pension under the provisions of section 2 of the
Local Offices Supn. Act 1869 and that if it
is desired by the Council to award him a retiring
allowance on the ground of health
under the provisions of Section 1
of the Act it will be necessary
for T.C.to be supplied with a
medical certificate testifying to his
permanent infirmity of mind or body
for the efficient discharge of the duties
of the post of Harbour Master
28/7/16
